zotero.abstractNote: We study the problem of identifying latent sources from multiple observed views, where each view is a nonlinear transformation of the same underlying sources. This is a generalization of Independent Component Analysis (ICA) to the nonlinear and multi-view setting. We show that, under certain conditions, the latent sources are identifiable up to a permutation and element-wise transformations. Our results provide a theoretical foundation for multi-view representation learning and suggest that the availability of multiple views can alleviate the fundamental non-identifiability of nonlinear ICA.
